comparisons.sharedLightWorld: soft clinic daylight with facade bounce and mild dust haze
decisionReasons: ensemble density is high enough that internal blocking is possible but not the default baseline
the daylight world remains stable across all route options

routeOptions.lightingHandling: full internal relight with ensemble protection lanes
riskNotes: careful crowd spacing required to avoid protection overlap

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight with optional environment polish
riskNotes: best default for moderate ensemble density

routeOptions.lightingHandling: reference the same daylight world in start/end frames
riskNotes: not preferred unless motion/camera complexity spikes

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: single warm horizon key with skyline rim and restrained bloom
decisionReasons: rim direction must stay stable across multiple performers
the sunset world is unified but ensemble staging adds density risk

routeOptions.lightingHandling: full internal relight with fixed horizon rim logic
riskNotes: highest risk is rim inconsistency across the line of performers

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus sky band polish
riskNotes: safest baseline for preserving sunset direction while softening density pressure

routeOptions.lightingHandling: sunset world referenced by packet and frames
riskNotes: fallback only for unusually complex choreography

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: cool moonlit street with sparse practical spill pools
decisionReasons: night practicals plus ensemble density make internal-only execution fragile
the same moonlit world can still anchor all route options

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal moonlight plus selective practical accent channels
riskNotes: high risk of readability loss in low-value beats

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus assisted practical spill polish
riskNotes: best compromise for continuity under crowd density

routeOptions.lightingHandling: moonlit world described through packet plus reference frames
riskNotes: acceptable when density and movement exceed hybrid envelope

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: overhead fluorescent pool with cool room bounce and limited local warmth correction
decisionReasons: the clinical overhead world is predictable even for moderate ensemble staging
density is manageable and practical variation is low

routeOptions.lightingHandling: full internal clinical relight with ensemble protection coverage
riskNotes: must avoid introducing a second light source

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus background polish
riskNotes: hybrid remains valid but should not alter fluorescent flatness

routeOptions.lightingHandling: fluorescent world referenced but fully regenerated
riskNotes: rarely justified because internal control is stronger here

comparisons.sharedLightWorld: soft courtyard daylight with warm wall bounce and controlled doorway contrast
decisionReasons: the courtyard profile is soft, stable, and package-friendly for a four-person held composition
there are few unstable practicals and the world key stays coherent

routeOptions.lightingHandling: full internal relight with support protection lanes
riskNotes: works well if support protection lanes remain explicit

routeOptions.lightingHandling: internal character relight plus courtyard atmosphere polish
riskNotes: useful if scene blocking widens beyond the pilot envelope

routeOptions.lightingHandling: courtyard world referenced by packet and frames
riskNotes: fallback only for exceptional action or crowd motion
